beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: At thirty-four, with family planning ahead, nine months of runway is a sprint, not a launchpad. What is our first revenue-generating experiment?

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: You think business feels easy because your side projects didn't have to buy baby formula; show me customers before you burn that runway.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: You're thirty-four, comfortable, and dying on the vine; jump now or admit you chose a safe life over your own freedom.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: You are thirty-four, planning a baby, and thinking a nine-month runway is enough to jump without a parachute? That is not freedom, it is terrifying.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old self, jumping blind isn't freedom—it's a trap. We will build this, but on a bridge, not a cliff.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: To the 18-year-old: Freedom is great, kid, but teenage angst doesn't pay for diapers. Show me paying customers, or keep your day job.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: Parents, you call nine months terrifying, but thirty more years of trading my freedom for a cubicle is what actually kills me.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old self, you don't have to pay for diapers; trading nine months of safety for "freedom" when a baby is coming is reckless.

beats.label: Verdict
beats.speaker: Council chair
beats.text: You have the fire and the runway, but jumping blind with a family on the horizon is a trap, not freedom.

verdict.decision: Transition to your own business, but only after securing your first paying customer while employed.
verdict.conditions: You must validate your product with one paying client and establish a strict family-approved emergency fund.
verdict.firstMove: Draft a pitch for your side project and send it to three potential warm leads today.

alignment.keyConcerns: Launching without a validated, revenue-generating business model first.
Confusing side-project hobbies with actual, diaper-paying market demand.
Delaying the leap and letting comfort kill the dream entirely.
Draining nine months of savings with a new baby arriving.
